task_summary: Add an audio analysis layer that extracts frequency/amplitude/beat data from all timeline audio tracks, expose it via a useAudioReactive() hook injected into runtime-compiled custom effects (via compileEffect.tsx's new Function() call), update the AI prompt template to document the new audio globals, and add an audio-binding parameter type to the ParameterControls system. The effect wrapping pipeline (wrapWithEffect/wrapWithClipEffects) must remain unchanged — audio data flows through the hook, not props.

key_evidence.point: compileEffect.tsx uses new Function() with 6 named parameters (React, useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, interpolate, spring, AbsoluteFill) at line 65-72. Adding useAudioReactive requires adding it as a 7th parameter to both the factory constructor and the factory() invocation at line 75.

No Remotion audio analysis packages are installed. Only @remotion/media (playback), @remotion/player, and @remotion/web-renderer are in package.json. @remotion/media-utils (which provides getAudioData/visualizeAudio) is NOT installed and would need to be added, OR a custom Web Audio API solution is needed.

AudioTrack.tsx renders each audio clip as a Remotion Audio element with src, volume, trim, and playbackRate. There is no audio analysis, mixing, or Web Audio API usage — purely playback. Audio clips come from ResolvedTimelineClip with assetEntry.src URLs.

ParameterType is a union of 'number' | 'select' | 'boolean' | 'color' defined in types/index.ts:6. Adding 'audio-binding' requires extending this union and adding corresponding UI in ParameterControls.tsx and validation in validateParams.ts.

effectPromptTemplate.ts is a simple template string listing available globals (line 20-26). The new useAudioReactive() hook needs to be documented here with its return type signature so AI-generated effects can use it.

The effect wrapping pipeline (wrapWithEffect at index.tsx:121-138, wrapWithClipEffects at index.tsx:140-189) passes props to EffectComponent. Task explicitly says this must remain unchanged — audio flows through the hook context, not props.
EffectComponentProps type is { children, durationInFrames, effectFrames?, intensity?, params? }. No audio-related props — confirming the hook-based approach is correct.

TimelineRenderer renders audio tracks separately from visual tracks (line 154-158). Audio clip data (src URLs, timing, volume) is available from config.clips filtered by audio track kind. The analysis layer needs access to this same resolved config to know which audio files to mix down.

EffectCreatorPanel.tsx uses a Remotion Player for preview (line 3) with a makePreviewComposition helper. Preview of audio-reactive effects will need either a mock audio signal or an actual audio source to be meaningful.
DynamicEffectRegistry compiles effects via compileEffect/compileEffectAsync. Both call tryCompileWithTransform which has the new Function() call. Only one code path needs modification for injection.

Continuous effects run for entire clip duration (effectFrames === durationInFrames). Entrance/exit effects have limited effectFrames. Audio reactivity is most natural for continuous but the hook should work in all categories since it reads from a context/global, not from effect-specific timing.

relevant_code.why: The new Function() call at line 65-75 is where useAudioReactive must be injected as a new global parameter
Must document useAudioReactive() in the Available Globals section so AI-generated effects can use audio data
ParameterType union (line 6) needs 'audio-binding' added; ParameterDefinition may need audio-specific fields
Must render new audio-binding parameter type UI (e.g., frequency band selector, amplitude mapping)
Must validate/coerce the new audio-binding parameter type
Current audio rendering — the analysis layer needs to work alongside or wrap this to tap into audio sources
Orchestrates visual+audio rendering. The AudioReactiveProvider context must wrap the composition here to make audio data available to all effects via the hook
Preview must support audio-reactive effects — may need mock audio data or an audio source picker
Must NOT be modified per task requirements — confirms audio flows via hook context, not props
Calls compileEffect/compileEffectAsync — no changes needed here since injection happens in compileEffect.tsx
Existing tests for effect compilation and registry — must continue passing after compileEffect changes
Effect layers also use wrapWithEffect — audio hook must work here too (confirmed: no changes needed since hook reads from context)
May need @remotion/media-utils dependency for getAudioData, or Web Audio API approach avoids new deps

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Effect compilation, registration, lookup, batching, parameter schema handling, error boundaries, useEffectRegistry hook integration
Hook returns frequency bins, amplitude, beat detection data; returns silent/zero data when no audio context; synchronized to current frame

test_expectations.test_id: new: compileEffect with audio global injection
test_expectations.what_it_checks: Compiled effects can call useAudioReactive() and receive audio data; existing effects without audio calls still work
ParameterControls renders audio-binding UI; validateParams handles audio-binding type; getDefaultValues returns sensible defaults
Provider extracts audio data from timeline audio tracks, mixes them, and provides frame-synchronized analysis data to descendant hooks

constraints: wrapWithEffect and wrapWithClipEffects in effects/index.tsx must remain unchanged — audio data flows through hook, not props
EffectComponentProps type should not gain audio-specific props — hook-based access only
Existing DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx tests must continue passing — compiled effects without audio calls must work identically
All existing built-in effects (11 entrance, 6 exit, 5 continuous) must be unaffected
Audio analysis must be synchronized to Remotion's frame clock (useCurrentFrame), not real-time wall clock
The hook must gracefully return zero/silent data when no audio tracks exist or audio context is unavailable
Preview in EffectCreatorPanel must work for audio-reactive effects (mock data or actual audio source)
Only @remotion/media is currently installed — any new Remotion packages need explicit addition to package.json
No SVG filters (blocked on cross-origin per effectPromptTemplate.ts constraints)
Custom effects use new Function() sandbox — no imports allowed, only injected globals
suggested_approach:
**Architecture: React Context + Web Audio API approach (no new Remotion packages needed)**

1. **AudioAnalysisProvider** (new file: `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/AudioAnalysisProvider.tsx`):
   - React context provider that wraps the composition in TimelineRenderer.tsx
   - Receives resolved audio clips (src URLs, timing, volume) from the timeline config
   - Uses Web Audio API to: fetch and decode all audio sources, mix them according to timeline positions/volumes, run an AnalyserNode to extract FFT frequency data and time-domain amplitude data per frame
   - Stores analysis results in a frame-indexed cache (pre-computed or computed on-demand)
   - Exposes data via context: `{ frequencyBins: Float32Array, amplitude: number, bass: number, mid: number, treble: number, isBeat: boolean, beatConfidence: number }`
   - Beat detection via simple energy threshold on low-frequency bins with cooldown

2. **useAudioReactive hook** (new file: `src/tools/video-editor/effects/useAudioReactive.ts`):
   - Reads from AudioAnalysisProvider context
   - Uses `useCurrentFrame()` to index into the frame-synchronized audio data
   - Returns `{ frequency, amplitude, bass, mid, treble, isBeat, beatConfidence }` for the current frame
   - Returns zeroed-out silent data when no provider exists (graceful fallback)

3. **compileEffect.tsx modification** (lines 65-75):
   - Add `'useAudioReactive'` as 7th parameter name in `new Function()` call
   - Pass the actual `useAudioReactive` function as the 7th argument in `factory()` call
   - This makes `useAudioReactive()` available as a global in all compiled custom effects

4. **effectPromptTemplate.ts update**:
   - Add `useAudioReactive()` to Available Globals section with return type documentation
   - Add usage examples showing frequency-driven scale, beat-driven flash, amplitude-driven opacity
   - Note that it returns silent data when no audio is present

5. **ParameterType extension** (types/index.ts + ParameterControls.tsx + validateParams.ts):
   - Add `'audio-binding'` to ParameterType union
   - Audio-binding parameter lets users map an audio channel (bass/mid/treble/amplitude) to a parameter with min/max range
   - ParameterControls renders: source selector (bass/mid/treble/amplitude/frequency-bin) + range slider
   - validateParams handles coercion of audio-binding values

6. **EffectCreatorPanel preview support**:
   - Wrap preview Player with AudioAnalysisProvider
   - For preview without real audio: provide a synthetic oscillating signal so audio-reactive effects show movement
   - Optional: allow selecting an audio file for preview

7. **TimelineRenderer integration** (minimal change):
   - Wrap the composition's AbsoluteFill with `<AudioAnalysisProvider clips={audioClips} fps={fps}>` so all visual effects can access audio data via the hook

**Key design decision**: Web Audio API for analysis (not @remotion/media-utils) because: (a) avoids new dependency, (b) gives full control over mixing multiple tracks, (c) AnalyserNode provides exactly the FFT/amplitude data needed, (d) can pre-compute per-frame data for deterministic renders.
